message.content:
You are doing a pure .adoc cosmetic edit on ONE file. No code, no behavior change, no semantic change. Mechanical apply only.

**File:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSRK-retriever_invest.adoc`

**Background:** Two changes — (A) reshape step 3 "Probe for Pre-existing Service Account" to match the parallel RBSDK form, and (B) convert the opener NOTE to line-broken style.

**Change A — Step 3 reshape (currently at L28-36):**

Current text:
```
//axhos_step
. *Probe for Pre-existing Service Account*:
.. {rbbc_call}
link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if HTTP 200: service account already exists — operator must run
{rbtgo_retriever_divest}
before re-investing
.. Continue only on HTTP 404 (account absent — proceed to create)
```

Replace with:
```
//axhos_step
. *Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist*:
.. {rbbc_call}
link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
.. {rbbc_fatal}
if HTTP 200: service account already exists — operator must execute
{rbtgo_retriever_divest}
before re-investing this role
.. {rbbc_require}
HTTP 404 to proceed
```

Diff summary: heading "Probe for Pre-existing Service Account" → "Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist"; "must run" → "must execute"; "before re-investing" → "before re-investing this role"; replace "Continue only on HTTP 404 (account absent — proceed to create)" with `{rbbc_require}` line + `HTTP 404 to proceed` line.

**Change B — Opener NOTE (currently at L4-7):**

Current text:
```
NOTE: This operation invests a {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} into the {rbtgi_depot_project} with read-only {rbtgi_repository} access.
The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_retriever}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_retriever_divest} first to re-key.
```

Replace with (each `{...}` linked term on own line; `${...}` shell-style refs stay inline since they're not AsciiDoc attribute refs):
```
NOTE: This operation invests a
{rbtr_retriever}
{giam_service_account}
into the
{rbtgi_depot_project}
with read-only
{rbtgi_repository}
access.
The
{rbtr_governor}
mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_retriever}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must
{rbtgo_retriever_divest}
first to re-key.
```

**DO NOT TOUCH:**
- The NOTE at step 2 "Derive Retriever Service Account Identity" (around L26) — has `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` in backticks but no `{...}` linked terms, leave alone
- The NOTE at "Verify Service Account Propagation" (around L60) — pure prose
- The NOTE at "Generate Service Account Key" (around L81) — only has `link:`, no `{...}` linked terms
- ANY other step content, //axhoot_typed_output, //axhoc_completion content
- Any `{rbbc_xxx}` constraint markers in OTHER steps
- Any other text in the file

**Method:**
1. Read the full file first
2. Use Edit tool to make exactly the two changes above
3. Read the file once more and verify ONLY those two regions changed
4. Report back what you changed

Report concisely: confirm the 2 edits, note any anomaly. Keep response under 150 words.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation invests a {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} into the {rbtgi_depot_project} with read-only {rbtgi_repository} access.
5	The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
6	The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_retriever}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
7	Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_retriever_divest} first to re-key.
8	
9	//axhos_step
10	. *Authenticate as
11	{rbtr_governor}*:
12	.. Execute
13	{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}
14	.. {rbbc_store}
15	`«GOVERNOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
16	
17	//axhos_step
18	. *Derive
19	{rbtr_retriever}
20	Service Account Identity*:
21	.. {rbbc_store}
22	`«RETRIEVER_ACCOUNT_NAME»` as `${RBCC_role_retriever}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (regime-kindled from
23	{rbrr_depot_moniker})
24	.. {rbbc_store}
25	`«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` as `«RETRIEVER_ACCOUNT_NAME»@${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com`
26	.. NOTE: `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` is derived from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)` at regime kindle time; no operator-supplied project ID field is referenced.
27	
28	//axhos_step
29	. *Probe for Pre-existing Service Account*:
30	.. {rbbc_call}
31	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
32	.. {rbbc_fatal}
33	if HTTP 200: service account already exists — operator must run
34	{rbtgo_retriever_divest}
35	before re-investing
36	.. Continue only on HTTP 404 (account absent — proceed to create)
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Create
40	{rbtr_retriever}
41	Service Account*:
42	.. {rbbc_call}
43	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/create[iam.serviceAccounts.create] with:
44	... accountId: `«RETRIEVER_ACCOUNT_NAME»`
45	... displayName: `Recipe Bottle Retriever`
46	... description: `Read-only access to Google Artifact Registry for depot ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`
47	... project:
48	{rbtgi_depot_project}
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	HTTP 200 response
51	.. {rbbc_store}
52	`«RETRIEVER_UNIQUE_ID»` from response `.uniqueId` field
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Verify Service Account Propagation*:
56	.. {rbbc_call}
57	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/get[iam.serviceAccounts.get] for `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
58	.. {rbbc_poll}
59	with backoff until HTTP 200 (newly-created SA may not be immediately resolvable by email)
60	.. NOTE: Poll must use the email address, not the numeric ID. The email-based lookup path has independent propagation from the UID-based path, and subsequent steps (keys.list, keys.create) all use the email address. Polling by UID can succeed while the email path still returns 404.
61	.. {rbbc_require}
62	service account accessible by email
63	
64	//axhos_step
65	. *Preflight: Ensure Pristine Key State*:
66	.. {rbbc_call}
67	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/list[serviceAccounts.keys.list] for `«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»`
68	.. {rbbc_store}
69	`«EXISTING_KEYS»` from response `.keys[]`
70	.. {rbbc_require}
71	no keys with `keyType: USER_MANAGED` exist
72	.. {rbbc_fatal}
73	if USER_MANAGED keys found: "Manual cleanup required via Console"
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Generate Service Account Key*:
77	.. {rbbc_call}
78	link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts.keys/create[serviceAccounts.keys.create] for `«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` with:
79	... privateKeyType: `TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE`
80	.. Retry up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` times on HTTP 404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts
81	.. NOTE: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) may succeed while keys.create POST returns 404 "does not exist" due to write-path propagation delay. Google link:https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[documents]: "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account." The 404 confirms no key was created, making retry safe. See <<rbscig_iam_grant_contracts>>.
82	.. {rbbc_store}
83	`«RETRIEVER_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` from response `.privateKeyData` field
84	.. {rbtoe_rbra_generate}
85	converts `«RETRIEVER_PRIVATE_KEY_DATA»` to `«RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE»`
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Grant
89	{rbtgi_repository}
90	Read Access*:
91	.. {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} for
92	{rbtgi_depot_project}
93	.. Add `serviceAccount:«RETRIEVER_EMAIL»` with role:
94	... `roles/artifactregistry.reader` (read-only access to all Artifact Registry repositories)
95	.. Policy version: 3
96	.. {rbbc_require}
97	IAM policy update successful
98	
99	//axhoot_typed_output
100	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
101	. *Deliver Credential to Assay Output Slot*:
102	.. {rbbc_store}
103	`«RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE»` RBRA bytes to the assay output slot via the producer-output discipline (the orphan's `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential write retires)
104	
105	//axhoc_completion
106	Successful execution invests retriever service account with read-only Artifact Registry access and delivers the RBRA credential bytes to the assay output slot.
107

content.text: Now I'll apply the two changes.

input.old_string:
NOTE: This operation invests a {rbtr_retriever} {giam_service_account} into the {rbtgi_depot_project} with read-only {rbtgi_repository} access.
The {rbtr_governor} mints the SA, generates its credential key, and writes the RBRA bytes to the assay output slot.
The operation is singleton-per-role: no instance argument; the SA identity is derived from regime constants as `${RBCC_role_retriever}-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}`.
Fatal if the SA already exists — operator must {rbtgo_retriever_divest} first to re-key.

content.text:
Both edits applied cleanly. No anomalies.

**Change A** (step 3): Heading renamed from "Probe for Pre-existing Service Account" to "Preflight: Confirm Service Account Does Not Exist"; "must run" → "must execute"; "before re-investing" → "before re-investing this role"; final sub-bullet replaced from plain prose to `{rbbc_require}` + `HTTP 404 to proceed`.

**Change B** (opener NOTE): Each `{...}` linked term broken to its own line; `${...}` shell-style refs left inline. No other text modified.
